steps.title: Find the central argument of the passage
steps.content: The passage opens by stating animals use objects as tools. It then names the old belief: tool use was thought unique to primates. The next two sentences refute that belief: dolphins use sponges to protect their noses, and palm cockatoos use leaves to open nuts. The passage's purpose is to show that a variety of non-primate animals use tools — overturning the primate-only assumption.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices and confirm D
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To question the belief that birds are intelligent': The passage never discusses intelligence; it calls cockatoo tool use "creative," implying the opposite of unintelligence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To compare tool use in humans with tool use in palm cockatoos': Humans are never directly mentioned as tool users in the passage; palm cockatoos appear as one example among several, not as a contrast to humans. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To establish that tool use doesn't indicate intelligence': Intelligence is never mentioned; the passage describes animal tool use as inventive and creative, not as evidence against intelligence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'To argue that a variety of animals use tools': The passage refutes the primate-only myth by showing that dolphins and palm cockatoos also use tools — exactly the argument that tool use spans a variety of animals. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
